instinct_candidates.title: Treat memory scope additions as contract changes
instinct_candidates.trigger: When adding or changing a memory scope kind in Loom memory.
instinct_candidates.action: Update the scope contract implementation in `src/agent_loom/memory/scopes.py`, then immediately align user-facing docs in `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md` and add/adjust focused regression tests so the new scope is exercised end-to-end.

instinct_candidates.title: Lock recall semantics with note-focused UX tests
instinct_candidates.trigger: When memory recall behavior or scope filtering changes.
instinct_candidates.action: Add or update high-signal recall-note tests in `tests/test_memory_recall_notes.py` to verify expected stdout/stderr semantics and avoid future drift in user-visible recall behavior.

instinct_candidates.title: Keep mirrored skill docs synchronized
instinct_candidates.trigger: When editing a skill that exists in both local `.opencode` and distributed `src/agent_loom/.../opencode` paths.
instinct_candidates.action: Update both copies in the same change set and verify they remain byte-for-byte aligned to prevent local/distributed behavior drift.

skill_candidates.name: loom-memory-scope-extension
skill_candidates.description: Procedure for adding or modifying Loom memory scope kinds without contract drift across code, docs, and recall UX tests.
skill_candidates.body:
# Loom Memory Scope Extension

Use this procedure whenever you add or change a memory scope kind.

## Steps
1. Update the scope contract in `src/agent_loom/memory/scopes.py`.
   - Add/adjust the canonical scope kind definition.
   - Ensure parsing/normalization/validation paths accept exactly the intended format.
2. Thread the change through recall behavior.
   - Confirm all scope-aware recall/filtering paths apply the new kind consistently.
3. Update docs in `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md`.
   - Document supported scope kinds and examples.
   - Keep wording aligned with actual CLI/runtime behavior.
4. Add regression coverage in `tests/test_memory_recall_notes.py`.
   - Add a positive case proving the new scope participates in recall as intended.
   - Add a negative/edge case that would fail if scope handling regresses.
5. If the skill is mirrored, sync both copies in one PR:
   - `.opencode/skills/loom-memory-scope-extension/SKILL.md`
   - `src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/skills/loom-memory-scope-extension/SKILL.md`

## Done Criteria
- Scope contract, recall behavior, docs, and tests all agree.
- Mirrored skill files (if present) are synchronized.
- No ambiguity remains about accepted scope syntax or recall outcomes.
